title: Infer latent tyre-degradation state and innovation shocks
summary: Recent F1 work models tyre pace as a latent state with pit-stop resets rather than relying only on observed cumulative degradation.
rationale: Recent F1 work models tyre pace as a latent state with pit-stop resets rather than relying only on observed cumulative degradation. For this task, the most useful signal may be not the latent level alone but the one-lap innovation: an unexpectedly bad lap given tyre age, compound, and race progress often precedes a stop.
implementation_hint: Fit a lightweight per-compound or per-race Kalman/state-space smoother offline on `LapTime (s)` or `LapTime_Delta` using `TyreLife`, `LapNumber`, `RaceProgress`, and reset on `PitStop==1`. Feed the classifier latent degradation estimate, filtered slope, innovation residual, and time-since-last-positive-innovation spike.
expected_effect: Captures hidden wear/thermal collapse that handcrafted window thresholds miss, especially when `Cumulative_Degradation` is noisy or race-specific.
risk: Too much per-race fitting can become brittle or leak if future laps inform current-row features; use causal filtering or train-only parameter estimation.
